UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents administrative users of the 'АльтернаТИВ АТС' application. Assumes external authentication system for credentials. Contains application-specific user data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user in the external authentication system. (Relationship: ExternalAuthSystem 1:N UserProfile)

email.description: The user's email address, used for display and communication, not for authentication.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'Admin', 'Operator').

preferences.description: A JSON string storing application-specific user preferences, such as UI settings or default views.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Extension.title: Extension
Extension.description: Represents an internal telephony extension (e.g., SIP/PJSIP endpoint) configured on the Asterisk server.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Extension entity.

extensionNumber.description: The actual internal dialing number for this extension.

description.description: A human-readable description or label for the extension.

deviceType.description: The type of telephony device protocol (e.g., 'PJSIP', 'SIP').

callerIdName.description: The caller ID name displayed when making outbound calls from this extension.

callerIdNumber.description: The caller ID number displayed when making outbound calls from this extension.

context.description: The Asterisk dialplan context associated with this extension.

secret.description: The authentication secret (password) for this extension. This field should be handled with extreme care regarding storage security (e.g., encryption at rest).

voicemailEnabled.description: Indicates if voicemail is enabled for this extension.

voicemailBox.description: The voicemail box number associated with this extension, if voicemail is enabled.

callForwardingEnabled.description: Indicates if call forwarding is enabled for this extension.

callForwardingDestinationExtensionId.description: Reference to the Extension entity to which calls are forwarded. (Relationship: Extension 1:N Extension)

musicOnHoldClass.description: The Music On Hold class used for this extension.

maxCalls.description: The maximum number of concurrent calls allowed for this extension.

recordCalls.description: Indicates if calls made or received by this extension should be recorded.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the extension was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the extension was last updated.

Trunk.title: Trunk
Trunk.description: Represents an external connection (e.g., SIP trunk) used for inbound and outbound calls to/from external telephony providers or other PBXs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Trunk entity.

name.description: A unique, human-readable name for the trunk.

description.description: A detailed description of the trunk's purpose or configuration.

host.description: The IP address or hostname of the remote telephony provider or PBX.

port.description: The port number used for the trunk connection.

username.description: The username for authenticating with the remote provider/PBX.

secret.description: The password or secret for authenticating with the remote provider/PBX. This field should be handled with extreme care regarding storage security (e.g., encryption at rest).

registerString.description: The full registration string required by Asterisk for this trunk, if applicable.

context.description: The Asterisk dialplan context for calls originating from or terminating on this trunk.

outboundCallerIdName.description: The caller ID name presented for outbound calls using this trunk.

outboundCallerIdNumber.description: The caller ID number presented for outbound calls using this trunk.

allowCodecs.description: An array of allowed audio codecs for this trunk (e.g., 'ulaw', 'alaw', 'g729').

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the trunk was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the trunk was last updated.

CallRoute.title: CallRoute
CallRoute.description: Defines rules for how incoming or outgoing calls are processed and routed within the Asterisk dialplan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CallRoute entity.

name.description: A unique, human-readable name for the call route.

description.description: A detailed description of the call route's purpose.

routeType.description: The type of call route ('Inbound' or 'Outbound').

priority.description: The order of evaluation for this route, lower numbers indicate higher priority.

pattern.description: The dial pattern (e.g., 'XNXXXXXXXXX' for outbound, or DID for inbound) that this route matches.

context.description: The Asterisk dialplan context where this route is active.

enabled.description: Indicates if the call route is currently active.

timeConditionId.description: Reference to a TimeCondition entity that must be met for this route to be active. (Relationship: TimeCondition 1:N CallRoute)

destinationExtensionId.description: Reference to an Extension entity if this route terminates at an internal extension. (Relationship: Extension 1:N CallRoute)

destinationQueueId.description: Reference to a Queue entity if this route sends calls to a call queue. (Relationship: Queue 1:N CallRoute)

destinationIvrId.description: Reference to an IVR entity if this route directs calls to an interactive voice response menu. (Relationship: IVR 1:N CallRoute)

destinationTrunkId.description: Reference to a Trunk entity if this route sends calls out through a specific trunk. (Relationship: Trunk 1:N CallRoute)

destinationVoicemailBox.description: The voicemail box number if this route directly sends calls to voicemail.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the call route was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the call route was last updated.

Queue.title: Queue
Queue.description: Represents an Asterisk call queue, managing incoming calls and distributing them to available agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Queue entity.

name.description: A unique name for the call queue.

description.description: A detailed description of the queue's purpose.

strategy.description: The ringing strategy for the queue (e.g., 'ringall', 'leastrecent', 'rrordered').

memberExtensionIds.description: An array of IDs referencing Extension entities that are members (agents) of this queue. (Relationship: Extension N:N Queue)

musicOnHoldClass.description: The Music On Hold class used when callers are waiting in the queue.

timeout.description: The maximum time (in seconds) an agent's phone will ring before timing out.

retry.description: The time (in seconds) to wait before retrying a queue member after a failed attempt.

wrapUpTime.description: The time (in seconds) an agent gets after a call before receiving another call from the queue.

joinAnnouncementFile.description: The filename of the audio announcement played when a caller joins the queue.

exitAnnouncementFile.description: The filename of the audio announcement played when a caller exits the queue.

maxWaitTime.description: The maximum time (in seconds) a caller will wait in the queue before being routed elsewhere.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the queue was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the queue was last updated.

Ivr.title: Ivr
Ivr.description: Represents an Interactive Voice Response (IVR) menu, guiding callers through options with digit presses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ivr entity.

name.description: A unique name for the IVR menu.

description.description: A detailed description of the IVR's purpose.

announcementFile.description: The filename of the audio announcement played as the IVR prompt.

timeout.description: The time (in seconds) to wait for user input before timing out.

invalidRetries.description: The number of times an invalid input prompt is played before exiting the IVR.

invalidAnnouncementFile.description: The filename of the audio announcement played on invalid input.

timeoutRetries.description: The number of times a timeout prompt is played before exiting the IVR.

timeoutAnnouncementFile.description: The filename of the audio announcement played on timeout.

digitMappings.description: An array of objects defining mappings from digit presses to destinations.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the IVR was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the IVR was last updated.

IvrDigitMapping.title: IvrDigitMapping
IvrDigitMapping.description: Represents a single digit-to-destination mapping within an IVR menu. This is a sub-entity for IVR digitMappings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IvrDigitMapping entity.

ivrId.description: Reference to the IVR entity this mapping belongs to. (Relationship: IVR 1:N IvrDigitMapping)

digit.description: The digit pressed by the caller (e.g., '1', '2'). Special values like 'i' for invalid input or 't' for timeout may also be used.

destinationExtensionId.description: Reference to an Extension entity if this digit routes to an extension. (Relationship: Extension 1:N IvrDigitMapping)

destinationQueueId.description: Reference to a Queue entity if this digit routes to a call queue. (Relationship: Queue 1:N IvrDigitMapping)

destinationIvrId.description: Reference to another IVR entity if this digit routes to a sub-IVR. (Relationship: IVR 1:N IvrDigitMapping)

destinationVoicemailBox.description: The voicemail box number if this digit routes directly to voicemail.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the IVR digit mapping was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the IVR digit mapping was last updated.

TimeCondition.title: TimeCondition
TimeCondition.description: Defines time-based rules for routing calls, allowing different destinations based on the time of day, day of week, etc.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimeCondition entity.

name.description: A unique, human-readable name for the time condition.

description.description: A detailed description of the time condition's logic.

timeStart.description: The start time for the condition in HH:MM format (e.g., '09:00').

timeEnd.description: The end time for the condition in HH:MM format (e.g., '17:00').

daysOfWeek.description: An array of numbers representing the days of the week (1=Monday, 7=Sunday) when the condition applies.

daysOfMonth.description: An array of numbers representing the days of the month (1-31) when the condition applies.

monthsOfYear.description: An array of numbers representing the months of the year (1-12) when the condition applies.

destinationTrueExtensionId.description: Reference to an Extension entity if the time condition is true. (Relationship: Extension 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationTrueQueueId.description: Reference to a Queue entity if the time condition is true. (Relationship: Queue 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationTrueIvrId.description: Reference to an IVR entity if the time condition is true. (Relationship: IVR 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationTrueVoicemailBox.description: The voicemail box number if the time condition is true and routes directly to voicemail.

destinationFalseExtensionId.description: Reference to an Extension entity if the time condition is false. (Relationship: Extension 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationFalseQueueId.description: Reference to a Queue entity if the time condition is false. (Relationship: Queue 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationFalseIvrId.description: Reference to an IVR entity if the time condition is false. (Relationship: IVR 1:N TimeCondition)

destinationFalseVoicemailBox.description: The voicemail box number if the time condition is false and routes directly to voicemail.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the time condition was created.

lastUpdateDate.description: Timestamp when the time condition was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID is the user's Firebase Auth UID. Users can manage their own profile; administrators can manage all. Includes 'externalAuthId' which maps to the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A control collection where the existence of a document signifies that the user (whose UID is the document ID) has administrative privileges. Used for Authorization Independence and efficient role checks in security rules. Implicitly derived from UserProfile's role, but denormalized for rule efficiency.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has been granted administrative privileges.

definition.description: Stores configurations for internal telephony extensions (SIP/PJSIP endpoints). Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: extensionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extension.

definition.description: Stores configurations for external telephony trunks (e.g., SIP trunks). Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: trunkId
params.description: The unique identifier for the trunk.

definition.description: Defines rules for how incoming or outgoing calls are processed and routed. Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: callRouteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the call route.

definition.description: Manages Asterisk call queues, including member extensions. Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: queueId
params.description: The unique identifier for the call queue.

definition.description: Stores Interactive Voice Response (IVR) menu configurations. Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: ivrId
params.description: The unique identifier for the IVR menu.

definition.description: Stores individual digit-to-destination mappings for a specific IVR menu. This is a subcollection of 'ivrs'. Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: ivrId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent IVR menu.

params.name: digitMappingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the IVR digit mapping.

definition.description: Defines time-based rules for call routing. Accessible by administrators for CRUD operations, and potentially operators for read-only access.

params.name: timeConditionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the time condition.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control following the mandated CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES. All administrative configuration data for the 'АльтернаТИВ АТС' application is stored in top-level collections, with user profiles and administrative roles managed distinctly.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User Profiles (`/user_profiles/{userId}`):** Each user's profile is stored with its document ID matching their `externalAuthId` (Firebase Auth UID). This allows users to read/update their own profile independently (`request.auth.uid == userId`) without requiring `get()` calls on other documents for basic authorization. Administrators can access all profiles.
2.  **Administrative Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** To achieve true authorization independence for admin checks, a dedicated, lightweight collection `/roles_admin/{userId}` is used. An administrator's UID is the document ID in this collection. The mere *existence* of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` immediately confirms the user's admin status in security rules, eliminating the need to fetch and inspect the potentially larger `UserProfile` document. This enables atomic and efficient role-based authorization for all system-level configuration entities.
3.  **Core Configuration Entities:** All other configuration entities (Extensions, Trunks, CallRoutes, Queues, IVRs, TimeConditions) are top-level collections. Their authorization is solely dependent on the user possessing the `AdminRole` (checked via `/roles_admin/{userId}`), not on any attributes of parent documents. This design ensures that creating, updating, or deleting these configuration items does not require complex multi-document reads in security rules.
4.  **Nested Data (`/ivrs/{ivrId}/digit_mappings/{digitMappingId}`):** `IvrDigitMapping` entities are placed in a subcollection under their respective `Ivr` parents. Their authorization also relies directly on the `AdminRole` status, making access independent of the parent `Ivr` document's specific attributes for rule evaluation.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** All collections, except `/user_profiles`, are designed to have a homogeneous security posture: they are primarily managed (CRUD operations) by users with the `AdminRole`, and potentially viewable (R operations) by an 'Operator' role (which would similarly be denoted by a dedicated `/roles_operator/{userId}` collection if needed, or by a check on `user_profiles.role` for less critical 'read-only' access). This segregation ensures that a `list` operation on any of these collections by an authorized user will always return the full set of accessible documents, without requiring client-side filtering or complex, inefficient rule-based filtering.
2.  For `/user_profiles/{userId}`, non-admin users can only `get` their own profile, making `list` operations effectively restricted to administrators. This strictly adheres to the QAP principle by preventing arbitrary `list` queries from returning unauthorized data or requiring rule-based filtering.
